blocks.text: Insert a flat-blade screwdriver into the notch on the solar radiation sensor (A), lift the solar radiation sensor slightly, and pull it out. Turn ignition to ON mode.

items: Do not scratch the instrument panel.
Minus Do not forcefully pry the driver.

blocks.text: Connect a (+) tester rod to the No. 2 terminal of the sunlight sensor and a (-) tester rod to the No. 3 terminal (for vehicles equipped with auto lights) or No. 1 terminal (for vehicles not equipped with auto lights), and check the voltage. Voltage changes cannot be confirmed by flashlights, fluorescent lights, or other light sources.

items.text: When the top of the sensor is covered by hand and there is no sunlight: 3.56V or higher.
When the top of the sensor is covered by hand and there is no sunlight: 3.56V or higher.
When the top surface of the sensor is fully exposed to sunlight: confirm that the voltage decreases from 3.56V.
When the top surface of the sensor is fully exposed to sunlight: confirm that the voltage decreases from 3.56V.
